full_narration: Did you know, Deception Island, a haven for whales, was also a site of unimaginable human depravity? In 1675, the Dutch East India Company's ship, the Gulden Zeepaert, was wrecked off its coast, killing 11 sailors. But that's not all, during WWII, the Allies used Deception Island as a secret military base, conducting experiments on prisoners of war. These experiments, though horrific, remain largely unspoken about to this day.
